title: G3 ungoverned-arch scope — making a newly-activated governance gate's 350-item pool actionable as a pre-flight signal
summary: Prep research for decision #1498. THE PROBLEM: repairing the dead `isExec` predicate (#1473) brought check:health's G3 (ungoverned-arch) from inert (0) to 350 candidates — every resolved build that graduated to an entity (`graduatedTo` set) with no governing `type:decision` reachable transitively (parent/blockedBy closure) nor cited in prose (we:scripts/audit-backlog-health.mjs:371). 350 is too voluminous to action as a pre-flight signal; #1498 must decide a scope. GROUNDING THE THREE NAMED OPTIONS AGAINST THE REAL DATA (1483 items, this tree): (A) RECENCY SCOPE is a FALSE PREMISE — the whole backlog's resolved history is compressed into June 2026 (1466 of 1483 items have dateOpened in 2026-06; the repo's first commit is 2026-01-23 but the items were bulk-created/migrated). Of the 350, only 253 are git-datable (a real `status:resolved` flip commit) and ALL 253 fall in 2026-06; the other 98 are import-born / born-resolved and carry NO usable date at all. Worse, the recent resolves DOMINATE the volume: 172 of the 253 datable resolved in the last three days (06-19..06-21), so 'scope to items resolved after the governance discipline began (platform-decisions.md, 2026-06-18 / first resolved decision 2026-06-06)' still leaves ~181 datable + 98 undatable ≈ 279. Recency cannot separate signal from noise here, and the 98 undatable can't be classified by it at all. EXTERNAL PRIOR ART explains WHY recency fails in this shape: SonarQube 'new-code period' / leak period, ESLint & Android-Lint & betterer BASELINE snapshots, and reviewdog filter-mode (added/file/nofilter) all gate only on code changed AFTER a clean forward boundary — they work because there is a real temporal boundary separating legacy from new. Our backlog has no such boundary (compressed history + import-born undatables), so the temporal discriminator collapses. (B) STRONGER LINEAGE FILTER, read literally as 'detect more governance edges', shaves almost nothing: only 11 of 350 mention platform-decisions.md in body, only 2 cite ANY decision (incl. open), 0 carry a codifiedIn frontmatter — the pool is genuinely arch with no decision in any form, not false-positives from a too-narrow lineage walk (#612 already hardened that walk). BUT reframed as a stronger SUBJECT filter it is the real fix: of the 350, 306 graduate to a FILE PATH (`scripts/autofix/engine.mjs`, `src/_includes/...` — routine impl landing in/creating a file) and only 45 graduate to a named `<kind>:` ENTITY, of which 41 are standard-entity kinds (block 27, intent 4, project 6, adapter 3, protocol 1; plug/capability 0) and 4 are `demo:`. The 41 entity-graduations are exactly the violation class G3 means to catch — new named standard entities (block:pagination #36, block:data-grid #123, block:dialog #376, intent:collection-operations #60, protocol:report-model #431, project:webcompliance #351, block:checkbox #472 …) that shipped with no governing decision; each is a real 'should this entity's existence/shape have been a decision?' card. The 306 file-path graduations are impl, not architecture — a file landing in an existing subsystem rarely needs a fresh decision — so counting them as 'ungoverned arch' is a SUBJECT category error, the root cause of the 42% over-fire (350/827 graduated builds). (C) FULL-POOL AUDIT-ONLY keeps all 350 as a report inventory but drops it from the pre-flight candidate count. WITHIN-REPO PRIOR ART, two strong precedents: (1) G6 codification-gap is explicitly 'the catch-up pool for the LEGACY decisions resolved before this gate existed — it should only ever shrink' (we:docs/agent/backlog-workflow.md:196): a gate activated late lights up the whole pre-discipline history, and the sanctioned treatment is to hold that history as a shrinking legacy inventory, not an action list. G3's 350 is the same phenomenon (a gate resurrected by #1473 over a history that predates the decision-as-work-item discipline). (2) The `--scope=<batch-slug>` changeset attribution (#957/#949, see research/gate-violation-attribution) already demotes pre-existing check:health findings to a non-failing note and surfaces only the session's claimed items — so within a batch the pre-flight actionability is ALREADY half-solved; the unscoped summary count is what still shows 350. The other CANDIDATE pools (G4/G5/G7) stay small precisely because they key on a NARROW subject (prepared decisions, codified cites); G3's subject (any graduated build) is the only over-broad one. SYNTHESIS handed to #1498: the recommended fix is the SUBJECT-scope reframe of option (B) — scope G3's predicate to entity-graduation (graduatedTo matches `^<kind>:` for a standard-entity kind, excluding file-path and `demo:`) → 350 drops to ~41, actionable; recency (A) is the data-disproven / fork-existence flawed branch; full-pool audit-only (C) is the coherent alternative but abandons the pre-flight value entirely, whereas (B) preserves it AND restores actionability, with the full 350 still retained in the report (the audit already displays/caps the list) as a G6-style shrinking legacy inventory. Residual ~20-25%: a genuine new subsystem shipped as a file-path graduatedTo (autofix engine #95, component-transform POC #48, plug runtime #1003) escapes the entity filter — accepted, caught by the judgment skim and normally governed one epic-hop up. Classification: repo-internal backlog devtools/workflow tooling, NOT a WE standard (impl-is-not-a-standard, minimize-lock-in) — no protocol minted; the reusable rule it would codify ('a governance gate's subject is governable architecture — a new named standard entity — not routine impl') is statute-layer material for platform-decisions.md or backlog-workflow.md on resolve. Full grounding numbers reproduced live from this tree via the G3 predicate; see reports/2026-06-21-g3-ungoverned-arch-scope.md if expanded.
